rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports belong in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annexes only in valid scopes matching component type or implementation.
Use only visible identifiers from the current AADL component scope in AGREE contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings must appear after relevant subcomponents and connections in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference event ports as numeric or Boolean values without model-provided data.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
